heading: Establishment of Advisory Commission on Accessible Instructional Materials in Postsecondary Education for Students with Disabilities
label: 1140l Establishment of Advisory Commission on Accessible Instructional Materials in Postsecondary Education for Students with Disabilities

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a commission to be known as the Advisory Commission on Accessible Instructional Materials in Postsecondary Education for Students with Disabilities (in this section referred to as the “Commission”).

children.heading: Total number of members
children.label: A Total number of members
children.text: The Commission shall include not more than 19 members, who shall be appointed by the Secretary in accordance with subparagraphs (B) and (C).

children.label: i
children.text: The Office of Postsecondary Education of the Department.

children.label: ii
children.text: The Office of Special Education and Rehabilitative Services of the Department.

children.label: iii
children.text: The Office for Civil Rights of the Department.

children.label: iv
children.text: The Library of Congress National Digital Information and Infrastructure Preservation Program Copyright Working Group.

children.label: v
children.text: The Association on Higher Education and Disability.

children.label: vi
children.text: The Association of American Publishers.

children.label: vii
children.text: The Association of American University Presses.

children.label: viii
children.text: The National Council on Disability.

children.label: ix
children.text: Recording for the Blind and Dyslexic.

children.label: x
children.text: National organizations representing individuals with visual impairments.

children.label: xi
children.text: National organizations representing individuals with learning disabilities.

children.heading: Additional members of the Commission
children.label: C Additional members of the Commission
i
children.text: Staff from institutions of higher education with demonstrated experience teaching or supporting students with print disabilities, including representatives from both two-year and four-year institutions of higher education of different sizes.

children.label: ii
children.text: Producers of accessible materials, publishing software, and supporting technologies in specialized formats, such as Braille, audio or synthesized speech, and digital media.

children.label: iii
children.text: Individuals with visual impairments, including not less than one currently enrolled postsecondary student.

children.label: iv
children.text: Individuals with dyslexia or other learning disabilities related to reading, including not less than one currently enrolled postsecondary student.

children.heading: Timing
children.label: D Timing
children.text: The Secretary shall appoint the members of the Commission not later than 60 days after the Commission is established under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Chairperson and vice chairperson
children.label: 3 Chairperson and vice chairperson
children.text: The Commission shall select a chairperson and vice chairperson from among the members of the Commission.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Commission shall meet at the call of the Chairperson.

children.heading: First meeting
children.label: B First meeting
children.text: Not later than 60 days after the appointment of the members of the Commission under paragraph (2)(D), the Commission shall hold the Commission’s first meeting.

children.heading: Quorum
children.label: 5 Quorum
children.text: A majority of the members of the Commission shall constitute a quorum, but a lesser number of members may hold hearings.

children.label: i
children.text: assess the barriers and systemic issues that may affect, and technical solutions available that may improve, the timely delivery and quality of accessible instructional materials for postsecondary students with print disabilities, as well as the effective use of such materials by faculty and staff; and

children.label: ii
children.text: make recommendations related to the development of a comprehensive approach to improve the opportunities for postsecondary students with print disabilities to access instructional materials in specialized formats in a timeframe comparable to the availability of instructional materials for postsecondary nondisabled students.

children.heading: Existing information
children.label: B Existing information
children.text: To the extent practicable, in carrying out the study under this paragraph, the Commission shall identify and use existing research, recommendations, and information.

children.label: I
children.text: to inform Federal regulations and legislation;

children.label: II
children.text: to support the model demonstration programs authorized under ;section 1140m of this title

children.label: III
children.text: to identify best practices in systems for collecting, maintaining, processing, and disseminating materials in specialized formats to students with print disabilities at costs comparable to instructional materials for postsecondary nondisabled students;

children.label: IV
children.text: to improve the effective use of such materials by faculty and staff, while complying with applicable copyright law; and

children.label: V
children.text: to modify the definitions of instructional materials, authorized entities, and eligible students, as such terms are used in applicable Federal law, for the purpose of improving services to students with disabilities.

children.label: aa
children.text: within a timeframe comparable to the availability of instructional materials for nondisabled students; and

children.label: bb
children.text: to the maximum extent practicable, at costs comparable to the costs of such materials for nondisabled students;

children.label: II
children.text: the feasibility and technical parameters of establishing standardized electronic file formats, such as the National Instructional Materials Accessibility Standard as defined in , to be provided by publishers of instructional materials to producers of materials in specialized formats, institutions of higher education, and eligible students;section 1474(e)(3) of this title

children.label: III
children.text: the feasibility of establishing a national clearinghouse, repository, or file-sharing network for electronic files in specialized formats and files used in producing instructional materials in specialized formats, and a list of possible entities qualified to administer such clearinghouse, repository, or network;

children.label: IV
children.text: the feasibility of establishing market-based solutions involving collaborations among publishers of instructional materials, producers of materials in specialized formats, and institutions of higher education;

children.label: V
children.text: solutions utilizing universal design; and

children.label: VI
children.text: solutions for low-incidence, high-cost requests for instructional materials in specialized formats.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 2 Report
children.text: Not later than one year after the Commission’s first meeting, the Commission shall submit a report to the Secretary and the authorizing committees detailing the findings and recommendations of the study conducted under paragraph (1).

children.label: A
children.text: the National Technical Assistance Center established under subpart 4; and

children.label: B
children.text: other means, as determined by the Commission.

children.heading: Termination of the Commission
children.label: c Termination of the Commission
children.text: The Commission shall terminate on the date that is 90 days after the date on which the Commission submits the report under subsection (b)(2) to the Secretary and the authorizing committees.
